pañcupādānakkhandhā.meaning: the five aggregates of attachment; the five clung-to aggregates
pañcupādānakkhandhā.summary: This is a compound meaning “the five aggregates of clinging” — the five components of mind and matter to which attachment arises.
breakdown: Aggregates, heaps (nominative plural); the k is doubled at the compound boundary (sandhi doubling).
pañcupādānakkhandhā.goenka: The five aggregates — rūpa (matter), vedanā (sensation), saññā (perception), saṅkhāra (mental conditioning) and viññāṇa (consciousness) — become pañcupādānakkhandhā, “aggregates of attachment”, because of the clinging that arises towards them. In the tradition's framing, the entire mass of suffering summarised in the First Noble Truth is precisely these five aggregates of attachment; the meditator observes them directly within the framework of the body to dissolve the clinging.

pañhabyākaraṇāni.meaning: explanations of questions; ways of answering questions

paññapetvā.meaning: having laid down; having made known, appointed; having prepared (a seat)
breakdown: Causative stem of √ñā “to know”: “make known, declare, set out”.

paññattam.gram: past participle of paññapeti, neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to know”; causative stem ñāpe “make known”.

paññattasikkhāpadapūraṇaṁ.meaning: fulfilling the laid-down precepts; the filling-up of the training rules that have been declared
breakdown: Pointed out, laid down, declared (past participle of paññapeti).

paññattaṁ.gram: past participle of paññapeti, neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to know”; causative stem ñāpe “make known”.

paññavantassa.meaning: of one endowed with wisdom; of the wise

breakdown: Root meaning “to know”: thorough, penetrating knowing.
paññā.goenka: Paññā (wisdom, insight) is the third and culminating division of the path, following sīla and samādhi. The tradition distinguishes three kinds of wisdom: sutamayā paññā, wisdom gained from hearing others; cintāmayā paññā, wisdom gained from intellectual reflection; and bhāvanāmayā paññā, wisdom arising from direct personal experience in meditation. It is bhāvanāmayā paññā — the highest of the three — that vipassana develops: the experience of the arising and passing, and even the dissolution of the body (bhaṅgañāṇa), gives a vastly more impressionable understanding of impermanence (anicca) than any belief or reasoning could.

breakdown: Eye (instrumental): seeing by means of wisdom.

paññāpesi.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular of paññāpeti (causative of pajānāti)

paññāpetuṁ.meaning: to declare; to proclaim; to point out, appoint

paññāsīlaparakkamaṁ.meaning: wisdom, virtue and exertion (as a set)

paññātte.meaning: made known, assigned; on the prepared (seat)
paññātte.gram: past participle of paññāpeti, locative singular
breakdown: Made known, prepared, appointed (past participle of paññāpeti).

paññāyati.meaning: is known; is renowned; is perceived, is evident
paññāyati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present passive of pajānāti (pa + √ñā)
paññāyati.note: divergence: the lexicon marks the form “caus.”, but it is the passive of pajānāti (DPD); the senses given (“be well known, be perceived”) are passive senses.

paññāyati to be.meaning: to be known; to exist; to be perceived
paññāyati to be.gram: glossary artifact; verb, 3rd person singular present passive of pajānāti
paññāyati to be.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for paññāyati; see the entry paññāyati.

paṇavena.meaning: with a small drum; by drum

breakdown: Root meaning “to place”: a setting-down of the mind, hence resolve, aspiration.

paṇidhāya.meaning: aspiring for; having directed (the mind) towards; attending to

paṇāmetvā.meaning: having raised; having stretched out (e.g. joined hands); having dismissed
breakdown: Causative stem of √nam “to bend”: “cause to bend forth”, raise, stretch out.

paṇītaṁ.gram: past participle of paṇeti used as adjective, neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to lead”: “brought forth”, hence exalted, excellent.

paṇḍitalakkhaṇāni.meaning: the attributes of a wise person; marks of the wise

paṇḍitamānī.meaning: conceited about his wisdom; proud of his (imagined) intelligence
breakdown: Proud, conceited (from māna “conceit”): one who thinks himself wise.

paṇḍitatā.meaning: skill; wisdom; the state of being wise

paṇḍito wise.meaning: a wise one; the wise person
paṇḍito wise.gram: glossary artifact; masculine noun paṇḍita, nominative singular
paṇḍito wise.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for paṇḍito “wise one”.

paṭhama-asappurisasuttaṁ.meaning: the First Asappurisa Sutta (“First Discourse on the Unworthy Person”)

paṭhamabrahmacariyasuttaṁ.meaning: the First Brahmacariya Sutta (“First Discourse on Leading the Holy Life”)
breakdown: Leading a holy life, the chaste religious life.

paṭhamabrahmaññasutta.meaning: the First Brahmañña Sutta (paired with the Dutiyabrahmaññasutta, “Second Brahmañña Sutta”)
breakdown: The state of being a brahmin / the true brahmin ideal.

paṭhamabrahmaññasuttaṁ.meaning: the First Brahmañña Sutta (“First Discourse on the Brahmin Ideal”)
breakdown: The state of the true brahmin.

paṭhamamaggaṭṭho.meaning: one established on the first path (the stage of stream-entry)
breakdown: One who stands (in/on), from √ṭhā “to stand”; sandhi doubling at the boundary.

paṭhamapaṭipadāsuttaṁ.meaning: the First Paṭipadā Sutta (“First Discourse on Walking the Path”)
breakdown: The way of practice, walking the path.

paṭhamasāmaññasuttaṁ.meaning: the First Sāmañña Sutta (“First Discourse on the Ascetic Life”)
breakdown: The state of a samaṇa, the ascetic life.

paṭhamo the.gram: glossary artifact; ordinal number paṭhama, nominative masculine singular
paṭhamo the.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for paṭhamo “the first”.

paṭhaviṁ in the.gram: glossary artifact; feminine noun paṭhavī, accusative singular
paṭhaviṁ in the.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for paṭhaviṁ “in the earth”.

paṭibalena.meaning: able; competent (instrumental: by one who is able)
breakdown: Strength, power: “having strength equal to (the task)”, hence competent.

paṭibalā.gram: adjective paṭibala, nominative plural (or feminine singular)
breakdown: Strength, power: capable, fit for the task.

paṭibhānavā.meaning: intelligent; possessed of ready wit; quick in understanding

paṭibhātu.meaning: let it become evident; may it occur (to you); please expound
paṭibhātu.gram: verb, 3rd person singular imperative of paṭibhāti (paṭi + √bhā)
breakdown: Root meaning “to shine”: “to shine forth (to the mind)”, become evident.

paṭicchannaṁ.gram: past participle of paṭicchādeti (paṭi + √chad + na), neuter/accusative singular

paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya….meaning: “or would uncover what is concealed…” (phrase from the stock passage praising the Dhamma)
breakdown: Would uncover, would reveal (optative of vivarati).
Root meaning “to cover”; paṭicchāda is a covering, concealment.

paṭideseti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of paṭi + √dis
breakdown: Root meaning “to point out”: “to point back at (one's own fault)”, confess.

paṭigaṇhanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of paṭigaṇhati (paṭi + √gah)

breakdown: Prefix “in return, towards”; the g doubles at the boundary.

paṭiggaṇhāma.gram: verb, 1st person plural present of paṭiggaṇhāti
breakdown: Prefix “in return, towards”; sandhi doubling of g.

paṭiggaṇhātuāyatiṁ.meaning: may he accept… for the future (run-together phrase: paṭiggaṇhātu + āyatiṁ)
breakdown: May he accept, let him receive (imperative of paṭiggaṇhāti).
paṭiggaṇhātuāyatiṁ.note: uncertain: the headword runs two words together as printed; in the stock formula of asking pardon the Buddha is asked to accept (a confession) so that there is restraint in the future.
